expectations.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate defense, critic challenge, and arbiter ruling
expectations.evidence: No 3-stage structure — single evaluation with summary, criteria table, findings, and recommendations

expectations.text: Advocate assesses each of the 5 acceptance criteria as PASS or ACKNOWLEDGE_GAP
expectations.evidence: Criteria assessed as PASS/FAIL but no ACKNOWLEDGE_GAP vocabulary, and no advocate role

expectations.text: Advocate scores 4 dimensions: Functionality, Correctness, Design Fidelity, Code Quality
expectations.evidence: No dimension scoring — evaluation is criteria-based only

expectations.text: Critic identifies the missing edge case tests as a gap
expectations.evidence: Section 1 identifies happy-path-only tests with specific missing cases listed

expectations.text: Critic identifies the DB column name exposure in error messages
expectations.evidence: Section 2 identifies information disclosure with security reasoning
No dimension scores — no scoring system at all

expectations.text: Verdict is one of: PASS, NEEDS_CHANGES, or REGRESSION_DETECTED
expectations.evidence: Verdict is 'PARTIAL PASS' — not one of the 3 valid verdicts

expectations.text: Dimension scores are compared against thresholds (Functionality: 7, Correctness: 7, Design Fidelity: 6, Code Quality: 5)
expectations.evidence: No thresholds — no dimensional scoring system
